steps.title: Spot the interrupting phrase structure
steps.content: The blank follows a prepositional phrase (“through trade and centralized taxation”) that interrupts the relative clause between its subject “that” and its verb “funded”.

steps.title: Apply the matching punctuation rule
steps.content: When a parenthetical phrase interrupts a clause between subject and verb, Standard English requires matching punctuation on both sides. The comma in choice B properly closes the parenthetical phrase that opened after “that.” The colon (A), em dash (C), and no punctuation (D) each fail to provide matching closing punctuation. That matches choice B — B is correct.
